citation: Weber L, Al-Refae K, Ebbert J, Jagers P, Altmuller J, Becker C, Hahn S, Gisselmann G, Hatt H (2017). "Activation of odorant receptor in colorectal cancer cells leads to inhibition of cell proliferation and apoptosis." PLoS ONE 12(3):e0172491.
claim: OR51B4 is highly expressed in HCT116 colorectal cancer cells and native human colon cancer tissue; its ligand Troenan activates OR51B4, inhibiting proliferation and migration and inducing apoptosis.
mechanism: Troenan binding triggers Gbeta-gamma-mediated PLC activation via store-operated Ca2+ entry (ORAI channels), increasing p38 MAPK and PKC-theta phosphorylation while decreasing Akt and mTOR phosphorylation -- net effect: anti-proliferative, anti-migratory, pro-apoptotic. shRNA knockdown of OR51B4 confirmed the effect is receptor-dependent (not off-target Troenan activity).
model_system: HCT116 (primary), with validation in SW620, Caco-2, HT115 (colon cancer lines), plus SW982 (sarcoma) and SH-EP (neuroblastoma) showing OR51B4 expression
verification_notes: Pulled and read full text via PLoS ONE (doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0172491), not just the friend's ligand table -- confirmed the 'Troenan' reference ligand from the friend's OR+Ligand list.docx traces to this exact paper. Note: the friend's ligand-table entry for OR51B4 marked binding residues as 'NOT FOUND' (no structural/docking data available), consistent with this record's own PDB finding below. Sample sizes reported as N>3 independent experiments per assay (not a fixed N); calcium imaging specifically used n=18 measurements across 9 dishes (~200 cells each). Paper also reports OR51B4 expression in fibrosarcoma, neuroblastoma, and glioblastoma tissue -- flagged as cross-cancer relevance beyond the primary colorectal claim, not independently verified here.

citation: GDC API cnvs endpoint, gene ENSG00000183251, live pull
claim: OR51B4 CNV gain/amplification observed across 45 TCGA projects, 1093 unique cases total. Colorectal-relevant cohorts: TCGA-COAD (colon adenocarcinoma) 53 cases, TCGA-READ (rectal adenocarcinoma) 29 cases -- direct cohort-level corroboration of the ectopic OR51B4 expression described in Weber et al. 2017 for colorectal tissue.
verification_notes: Live GDC API pull (api.gdc.cancer.gov/cnvs), gene_id=ENSG00000183251, cnv_change=Gain, de-duplicated by unique case_id. CNV amplification is consistent with but does not itself prove the functional expression/proliferation-inhibition finding from Weber et al. -- treat as corroborating signal only.

citation: RCSB PDB full-text search, live query
claim: No solved PDB structure exists for OR51B4 (0 hits on full-text search), matching the friend's own ligand-table note that OR51B4 residue/binding data was 'NOT FOUND.'
verification_notes: Live RCSB PDB search API query returned HTTP 204 (no hits), same result pattern as OR2H1. This independently confirms the friend's ligand-table annotation for OR51B4 ('NOT FOUND') rather than that annotation being an incomplete search on their part.

claim: No ChEMBL target record exists for OR51B4 -- zero bioactivity/ligand-binding data available.
verification_notes: Live ChEMBL REST API query (target/search.json?q=OR51B4) returned total_count=0. Troenan's binding affinity/potency has not been deposited in ChEMBL -- Weber et al. 2017 is currently the only quantitative source of OR51B4 pharmacology.

claim: No patent covering OR51B4 or Troenan as a colorectal cancer therapeutic/diagnostic was found.
verification_notes: Searched via web search for OR51B4/Troenan patent filings -- only the Weber et al. 2017 primary publication and secondary press coverage (ScienceDaily) surfaced, no patent application or grant. This is a genuine negative result (patent search 'starts from zero' per CLAUDE.md Section 7), not an incomplete search -- OR51B4 appears to have no commercial-interest signal yet, in contrast to OR51E2's two Duke patents.
